title: Kinder Morgan's Moving in the Right Direction, but Short of This 2020 Dividend Goal
text: In late 2015, Kinder Morgan told inventors to expect a dividend increase in 2016, but it cut the dividend instead. In 2017, the company discussed a long-term plan to increase the dividend, including a 25% increase in 2020 that didn't come to pass.

text: Given the current uncertainty, I believe high-quality, favorably valued, and low-risk investments warrant increased attention. Some midstream energy companies boast durable revenue, profitability, and well-supported high dividend yields.

title: Here's Why Hold Strategy is Apt for Kinder Morgan (KMI) Stock
text: Kinder Morgan (KMI) generates stable fee-based revenues from its vast network of midstream infrastructure.

title: Kinder Morgan Is Fixing Its Reputation, Buy The Dip
text: Kinder Morgan, Inc. has an impressive portfolio of natural gas assets, a fuel with much longevity. The company has a dividend yield of more than 6.5% along with a fixed capital stack and continued shareholder returns.

title: Why These Energy Stocks Have the Potential to Thrive
text: Natural gas will be an essential fuel to help compensate for renewable energy's intermittency issues. Kinder Morgan's extensive infrastructure gives it the flexibility to deliver natural gas when it's needed.

title: Kinder Morgan Remains Resilient Despite A Turbulent Environment
text: Markets have become volatile, and with rates continuing to increase, they will continue to be volatile. Kinder Morgan is an important part of the energy ecosystem.

title: Kinder Morgan: Years Of Disappointment Could Finally End After 2023
text: Ever since Kinder Morgan cut their dividends back in early 2016, the company has struggled to gain traction given subsequent disappointments. In my view, these have hindered their share price as the market questions their dividend growth potential.

title: Kinder Morgan: This 6.3% Yielding Pipeline Goliath Is Undervalued
text: Kinder Morgan, Inc. produced a solid 2022, and its guidance for 2023 is strong, leaving me hopeful that increased shareholder value is around the corner. Since 2016, Kinder Morgan has grown its quarterly dividend by 117.31%, from $0.13 to $0.2825, and it is yielding 6.3%, well over CDs and T-Bills.

title: This Dividend Stock Has Up to 25% Upside, According to Wall Street
text: Kinder Morgan has outperformed during the bear market. Analysts see even more upside ahead for the natural gas infrastructure giant.

title: Better High-Yield Midstream Buy: Kinder Morgan Or Enbridge?
text: Enbridge and Kinder Morgan are two high-yield midstream businesses. Both boast investment-grade credit ratings and very strong business models.

title: Kinder Morgan: Have Your Gas Man Pay You Back
text: Kinder Morgan has mission-critical energy infrastructure and is seeing strong growth. It has ample excess cash flow after covering the dividend, with which it can invest in low carbon and carbon capture projects.

title: 3 High-Yield Infrastructure Stocks to Buy in March
text: Brookfield Infrastructure has steadily raised its distribution higher over the past 10 years, and it doesn't plan on stopping that trend anytime soon. Caterpillar has plenty of potential for long-term dividend growth.

title: Dividend Income Summary: Lanny's February 2023 Summary
text: Investing in dividend stocks allows you to earn dividend income, the best passive income stream. As I stated in my December 2022 post, 2022 finished off down, ending up below 3,900! The market was down 20% for 2022.

title: 3 Oil Pipeline Stocks to Gain From the Thriving Industry
text: Since midstream operations have lower exposure to volatility in commodity prices, the outlook for the Zacks Oil and Gas - Production & Pipelines industry is bullish. Kinder Morgan (KMI), Williams Companies (WMB) and MPLX LP (MPLX) are the frontrunners in the industry.
